---
title: Employment recovery remains uneven especially in rural areas
description: Faceted line chart showing sectoral employment changes in tradable goods
  and tradable services since the Great Recession.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-2-2a-line-chart-change-in-wage-sector-facet/
categories:
  - Chart
  - Labor Market
  - Economic Indicators
tags:
  - QCEW
  - Employment
  - Sectoral Analysis
  - Tradable Goods
  - Tradable Services
  - Line Chart
image: 2.2A_line_chart_change_in_wage_sector_facet.svg
chart:
  title: Employment recovery remains uneven especially in rural areas
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  dataSource: Bureau of Labor Statistics, Quarterly Census of Employment and Wages
  sourceUrl: https://www.bls.gov/cew/
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - qcew-employment-wages
  - bls-cpi-deflators
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![This chart compares employment recovery across tradable goods and tradable services sectors for rural and nonrural areas since 2007.](./2.2A_line_chart_change_in_wage_sector_facet.svg)

## Overview

This chart compares employment recovery across tradable goods and tradable services sectors for rural and nonrural areas since 2007.

## Key Findings

- Tradable goods employment has declined in both rural and nonrural areas
- Tradable services employment shows stronger recovery in nonrural areas
- Rural areas face structural employment challenges across both sectors

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/sector_job_changes_2A.R` in the producing project.


::: {.callout-note}
## Dangling references

The following slugs are referenced by this project but do not yet have nodes in Dataverse. They are intentionally preserved as future content needs:

- `dataset/bls-cpi-deflators`
:::
